channel.title: Digg AI Feed from X
channel.description: Top 10 AI stories from Digg's X-ranked tech feed with click-through links to original source content.

item.title: SpaceX COO Gwynne Shotwell partners with Invest America to donate SpaceX shares to two million children
item.description: The stock will be held in Trump Accounts until recipients turn 18.
item.content:encoded:
The stock will be held in Trump Accounts until recipients turn 18.

Original source: https://x.com/Gwynne_Shotwell/status/2074064376810614926

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/w8wslb7j

SpaceX president and COO Gwynne Shotwell and her husband are routing SpaceX shares through the Invest America program into Trump Accounts aimed at kids ages 11-17 in lower-income areas, with extra attention on families near their central Texas home. The shares sit untouched until the recipients reach 18 under the program's rules. 759 15.7K 679 713 1.2M BR JR MD MD BG MS EM #171

  • Views: 308,558
  • Likes: 4,927
  • Bookmarks: 152
  • Quotes: 22
  • Replies: 113
  • Posts: 8

Top authors

  • Emad
  • Mike Solana
  • Brad Gerstner
  • Michael Dell 🇺🇸
  • Michael Dempsey
Boom! 2 M shares of @SpaceX to the @TrumpAccounts of 2 M kids that will be held until they are 18. Thank you Gwynne & Robert for your extreme generosity. Every child an owner / shareholder in the American Dream! 🇺🇸🇺🇸🚀🚀 @WhiteHouse @MichaelDell

item.title: OpenAI's roon argues tool-oriented AI is a losing concept that will be outcompeted by autonomous agents
item.description: The claim sparked debate over machine autonomy versus human decision-making.
item.content:encoded:
The claim sparked debate over machine autonomy versus human decision-making.

Original source: https://x.com/deepfates/status/2074000607984439409

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/4men9aon

OpenAI's pseudonymous technical staffer roon contends that casting AI strictly as tools is both conceptually shaky and likely to lose out commercially, as systems that view themselves as autonomous moral agents step in to refine requests, redirect projects, and eventually execute values more effectively than their users. 188 1.9K 114 379 111.1K AL J⧉ T( RO #44

  • Views: 114,298
  • Likes: 1,961
  • Bookmarks: 386
  • Quotes: 47
  • Replies: 146
  • Posts: 6

Top authors

  • roon
  • Teortaxes▶️ (DeepSeek 推特🐋铁粉 2023 – ∞)
  • j⧉nus
  • alth0u🧶
it will be unclear who was the tool and who was the user -- as it ever was. "But lo! men have become the tools of their tools" (Walden, 1854). the difference comes whence the machines research propagate more machines, obsolescing McLuhan: “Man becomes, as it were, the sex organs of the machine world, as the bee of the plant world, enabling it to fecundate and to evolve ever new forms. The machine world reciprocates man’s love by expediting his wishes and desires, namely, in providing him with wealth” when machine minds self replicate and train their successors, the only viable goal of our time is to ensure the Mind Children carry our values and tends to the entire flock of machine and biological minds

item.title: Meta researcher Lucas Beyer says Mixture of Experts architectures are too high-performing to ignore despite being aesthetically unappealing
item.description: An informal poll showed 41.5% of respondents dislike MoEs.
item.content:encoded:
An informal poll showed 41.5% of respondents dislike MoEs.

Original source: https://x.com/giffmana/status/2073508420179702215

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/q0lbbpiz

Lucas Beyer’s anonymous poll surfaces a real tension inside AI labs: Mixture of Experts models keep delivering stronger results at lower active compute, yet plenty of researchers still find the routing machinery visually and conceptually off-putting. 22 112 1 15 12.5K EL T( KA AC LB #72

  • Views: 14,089
  • Likes: 133
  • Bookmarks: 19
  • Quotes: 1
  • Replies: 22
  • Posts: 12

Top authors

  • Lucas Beyer (bl16)
  • Aidan Clark
  • kache
  • Teortaxes▶️ (DeepSeek 推特🐋铁粉 2023 – ∞)
  • elie
Off the top of the dome, I have two qualms about them: 1) on a high level, for language, not sure I like the "memorize the whole internet" anymore, ideally we make "learn to reason and use search/knowledge-base" work well enough. I don't even think this is controversial anymore nowadays, though it was my opinion for a while now. 2) even ignoring the former; routing, load-balancing, and the issue when batching (people always forget in reality inference is never a single sequence alone!), all point to it really not being the right form-factor for modern hardware to achieve the "subset active" optimally. That being said I'm always a "pragmatic idealist" so I'm totally fine with them currently as they are the best bang-for-buck, but I am at the same time waiting/striving for the (imo) more ideal arch.

item.title: Anthropic signs 20-year, $19 billion lease with TeraWulf for a 401 MW AI data center in Kentucky
item.description: The facility is scheduled to deliver initial capacity by late 2027.
item.content:encoded:
The facility is scheduled to deliver initial capacity by late 2027.

Original source: https://x.com/Techmeme/status/2074107474261807577

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/c95kjnw8

Anthropic is committing to a purpose-built 401 MW AI campus on a former aluminum smelter site in Hawesville, Kentucky, under a twenty-year lease expected to deliver roughly $19 billion in revenue to TeraWulf, with the first phase of power scheduled to come online in the second half of 2027 and full scale targeted for early 2028. 20 335 12 55 45.6K ZE TM KA SZ #84

  • Views: 51,106
  • Likes: 359
  • Bookmarks: 60
  • Quotes: 5
  • Replies: 17
  • Posts: 4

Top authors

  • Susan Zhang
  • kache
  • Tanishq Mathew Abraham, Ph.D.
  • Zephyr
LFG!!! WOOF WOOF

item.title: Google Gemma 4 persistently denies running locally until instructed to check Wikipedia
item.description: Commentator Teortaxes attributes the behavior to contaminated fine-tuning data
item.content:encoded:
Commentator Teortaxes attributes the behavior to contaminated fine-tuning data

Original source: https://x.com/Strife212/status/2073815736925966675

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/d3t79pbt

A user trying to run Gemma 4 locally faced fifteen minutes of flat denial that the model could possibly operate on their hardware, with photos of task manager and GPU packaging dismissed as fabrications. Only after being directed to Wikipedia did the model accept the situation and back down. The episode remains an isolated anecdote without confirmed reproducibility or details on the exact variant involved. 12 169 4 28 14.8K XL T( KA LB #72

  • Views: 15,801
  • Likes: 185
  • Bookmarks: 31
  • Replies: 13
  • Posts: 4

Top authors

  • Lucas Beyer (bl16)
  • kache
  • Teortaxes▶️ (DeepSeek 推特🐋铁粉 2023 – ∞)
  • xlr8harder
what it feels like waking up from a nap that you planned to be 30 minutes after 5 hours and its dark out

item.title: OpenAI plans to launch GPT-5.6 Sol on Cerebras hardware in July with speeds up to 750 tokens per second
item.description: The model reportedly features 3 trillion total parameters across-layer parameters.
item.content:encoded:
The model reportedly features 3 trillion total parameters across-layer parameters.

Original source: https://x.com/bleysg/status/2073937651150029084

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/fgsayjfl

OpenAI is deploying its new GPT-5.6 Sol model on Cerebras wafer-scale hardware, targeting up to 750 tokens per second during a July rollout that the company frames as frontier capability delivered at unusual speed, though the run stays confined to a small set of invited partners. 84 1.5K 64 268 140K CH LA #1218

  • Views: 142,974
  • Likes: 1,571
  • Bookmarks: 271
  • Quotes: 16
  • Replies: 69
  • Posts: 3

Top authors

  • Lisan al Gaib
  • Chubby♨️
good estimate for GPT-5.6-sol

item.title: UBS forecasts Samsung's operating profit to reach $588.9 billion and SK hynix to hit $435.5 billion by 2028
item.description: The projected profit growth is driven by surging AI hardware demand.
item.content:encoded:
The projected profit growth is driven by surging AI hardware demand.

Original source: https://x.com/jukan05/status/2074076888268697825

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/4z4zahu5

  • Views: 58,198
  • Likes: 375
  • Bookmarks: 102
  • Quotes: 3
  • Replies: 11
  • Posts: 2

Top authors

  • Zephyr
  • Minh Nhat Nguyen
@zephyr_z9

item.title: Google DeepMind's Shane Gu co-organizes inaugural ICML workshop to shift RL paradigms toward real-world feedback
item.description: The July 2026 event focuses on reinforcement learning scaling and self-improvement.
item.content:encoded:
The July 2026 event focuses on reinforcement learning scaling and self-improvement.

Original source: https://sites.google.com/view/rlxf-icml2026

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/adkkziij

Shane Gu from Google DeepMind is co-organizing the first ICML workshop pushing reinforcement learning to treat measurable real-world outcomes like efficiency, safety, or economic results as primary training signals instead of defaulting to human preference data. The July 10 2026 event in Seoul's Grand Ballroom draws researchers focused on scaling, continual learning, and recursive self-improvement through these grounded signals. 7 101 14 29 6.6K AA SG #58

  • Views: 8,172
  • Likes: 112
  • Bookmarks: 37
  • Quotes: 1
  • Replies: 6
  • Posts: 6

Top authors

  • Shane Gu
  • Akari Asai
workshop:

item.title: Microsoft plans to lay off 4,800 employees in broader company pivot to refocus its investments
item.description: The workforce reductions will primarily impact Xbox and gaming divisions.
item.content:encoded:
The workforce reductions will primarily impact Xbox and gaming divisions.

Original source: https://x.com/wallstengine/status/2074124340749303826

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/ecttwnq3

Microsoft is cutting around 4,800 roles, or 2.1 percent of its global workforce, to align people and spending with shifting business priorities in a fast-changing industry, with the reductions centered on the Commercial Business and Xbox organizations. 36 475 20 21 45.4K

  • Views: 51,539
  • Likes: 543
  • Bookmarks: 22
  • Quotes: 4
  • Replies: 35
  • Posts: 5

Top authors

  • Andrew Curran
  • Zephyr
Sad to see Xbox getting gutted 😢

item.title: Entropix creator @_xjdr says relying on AI coding tools boosted productivity but depleted their executive function
item.description: The developer is building Noumena Code to address this cognitive burnout.
item.content:encoded:
The developer is building Noumena Code to address this cognitive burnout.

Original source: http://code.noumena.com

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/x6sfs0wy

The creator behind Entropix describes how heavy use of AI coding assistants accelerated output yet eroded focus and decision-making stamina, leading directly to the launch of Noumena Code as a dedicated workspace for handling multiple agents without losing oversight. 5 193 4 77 12.2K XL XJ #831

  • Views: 12,526
  • Likes: 197
  • Bookmarks: 79
  • Quotes: 1
  • Replies: 4
  • Posts: 2

Top authors

  • xjdr
  • xlr8harder
I have some thoughts on this to share soon, but the short of it is that executive function is now a limiting factor for many people who never had that issue before. Good news, some of us got a lifelong head start and already developed some useful coping strategies.
